channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Richard Lewer Wins 2026 Archibald Prize for Portrait of Iluwanti Ken
item.description: The 2026 Archibald Prize, Australia’s most prestigious portrait award, has been awarded to Richard Lewer for his portrait of Pitjantjatjara elder and artist Iluwanti Ken. Lewer, a New Zealand-born, Me...

item.title: Companies Plan to Apply for Tariff Refunds After Supreme Court Ruling
item.description: Following a Supreme Court decision that deemed certain tariffs imposed under the Trump administration illegal, several major companies, including Philips and Pandora, have announced plans to seek refu...

item.title: Bonnie Tyler placed in induced coma following successful emergency intestinal surgery
item.description: Welsh singer Bonnie Tyler, aged 74, has been placed in an induced coma to support her recovery after undergoing emergency intestinal surgery in Faro, Portugal, where she has a home. The procedure took...

item.title: Cruise Ship With Hantavirus Cases Heads to Canary Islands Amid Evacuations and Monitoring Efforts
item.description: A cruise ship operated by Oceanwide Expeditions, the MV Hondius, is sailing toward Spain’s Canary Islands after reporting a hantavirus outbreak that has led to multiple deaths and medical evacuations....

item.title: Dar-Zanzibar ferry fares increase following rise in fuel costs
item.description: Ferry fares between Dar es Salaam and Zanzibar have gone up as fuel prices continue to rise, pushing operating costs for ferry companies higher. Economy class tickets now cost Sh40,000, up from Sh35,0...

item.title: Students and parents protest for reopening of sealed seminary-cum-school in Shopian, Kashmir
item.description: In Shopian, south Kashmir, students and parents of the Siraj-ul-Uloom seminary-cum-school staged a protest on May 7, 2026, demanding the reopening of the institution that was closed under the Unlawful...

item.title: Anniversary of India-Pakistan Marka-i-Haq Conflict and Drone Interceptions
item.description: The week of May 6 to May 10 marks the first anniversary of the short but intense military clash between Pakistan and India, which escalated due to the Pahalgam attack on tourists in Indian-administere...

item.title: Tamil Nadu Class 12th Results 2026: Official Updates and Scorecard Download Information
item.description: The official results for Tamil Nadu's Class 12 examinations for the year 2026 are scheduled to be announced online by the Directorate of Government Examinations (DGE). Students who appeared for the Hi...

item.title: High Court Allows Key Affidavit in Gachagua Impeachment Case, Sets Stage for Further Scrutiny
item.description: Former deputy president Rigathi Gachagua has secured an early legal win in his ongoing impeachment case after the High Court allowed a contested affidavit to remain part of the court record. The affid...

item.title: India Rejects Canadian Report Alleging Foreign Interference
item.description: On Thursday, the Government of India firmly rejected a report by the Canadian Security Intelligence Service (CSIS) that alleged foreign interference by India. The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) cl...

item.title: Surgeon faces manslaughter charges after patient dies from surgical error
item.description: Dr Thomas Shaknovsky, a surgeon from the United States, is facing a manslaughter charge following the death of Bill Bryan, a 70-year-old man from Alabama, in August 2024. Bryan, who was on holiday wit...

item.title: Amanda Abbington's fiancé receives compensation after stunt accident on America's Got Talent
item.description: Amanda Abbington and her fiancé Jonathan Goodwin have reached a confidential settlement following the life-changing accident Goodwin suffered during rehearsals for America's Got Talent: Extreme in 202...

item.title: Nishant Kumar and 31 Ministers Take Oath in Bihar Cabinet Under CM Samrat Choudhary
item.description: Nishant Kumar, the son of former Bihar Chief Minister Nitish Kumar, officially entered politics in March this year by joining Janata Dal (United) and was sworn in as a minister in the BJP-led Bihar Ca...

item.title: Union Government Introduces Annual Free Health Checkups for Workers Over 40
item.description: On Thursday, the Union Government of India rolled out a nationwide initiative offering free annual health check-ups for all workers aged 40 years and above, under the recently notified labour codes of...

item.title: Federal Government Moves to Evict Penticton Shooting Club Amid Land Reclamation Plans
item.description: After over four decades of operation, the Penticton Shooting Club in British Columbia is facing eviction from its longstanding property owned by Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada. The federal governmen...

item.title: Makueni student appeals for support after missing school due to unpaid fees

item.title: US Lawmakers Demand Clarity on Israel’s Nuclear Capabilities Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Nearly 30 Democratic lawmakers in the United States have urged Secretary of State Marco Rubio to clarify Washington’s long-standing policy of ambiguity regarding Israel’s nuclear weapons programme. Th...

item.title: Uber projects solid Q2 growth driven by delivery demand and platform expansion despite geopolitical headwinds
item.description: Uber has projected stronger-than-expected gross bookings for the second quarter, signalling continued demand for its ride-hailing and delivery services even as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East...

item.title: Kate Middleton Participates in Early Childhood Research at University, Highlighting Practical Impact of Her Advocacy
item.description: On May 6, 2026, Kate Middleton, the Princess of Wales, visited the University of East London to engage directly with early childhood research. During her visit, she interacted with families, students,...

item.title: Trump Administration Proposes USPS Policy Allowing Handguns to Be Mailed Within States
item.description: The Trump administration is advocating for a policy change that would allow people to send handguns through the United States Postal Service (USPS) after the Department of Justice declared the nearly ...
